text:
3D platformer Garfield: Escape from Monday announced for Nintendo Switch [update: trailer]

May 13: Microids, along with OSome Studio, today unveiled Garfield: Escape from Monday for Nintendo Switch. The game is due out on September 24, 2026. Garfield: Escape from Monday is a “veggie filled” 3D platformer. In it, players will need to help the tabby cat wake up from his nightmare by exploring different worlds. text:

According to LightWitch and Blue Lily’s latest announcement, Moonlight Pale is making its way to Nintendo Switch. The team aims to release its game between Q4 2026 and Q1 2027. SEGA is putting Shinobi: Art of Vengeance on Nintendo Switch 2, the company just announced. Fans will be able to grab this version on September 24, 2026. text:
Arcade game Biomechanical Toy to hit Nintendo Switch

QUByte Interactive made it known today that it’s working on an arcade port of Biomechanical Toy for Nintendo Switch. It’ll be out on July 16, 2026. Gaelco was behind the original debut of Biomechanical Toy in 1995. For this release, fans can look forward to rewind functionality and save states plus the ability to view the original artwork and access...

text:

As announced by QUByte today, Glover and Street Racer Collection will both be receiving free updates on Nintendo Switch to add their PlayStation versions. text:

Today, QUByte Interactive unveiled a trio of future PlayStation 1 games releasing on Nintendo Switch. Invasion from Beyond, Motor Mash, and Tigershark are all lined up for a 2027 launch. text:
Gaelco Sports Collection, Thunder Hoop Collection announced for Nintendo Switch

QUByte Interactive is making Gaelco Sports Collection and Thunder Hoop Collection for Nintendo Switch, the company revealed today. A release is scheduled for 2026 both both packages. Gaelco Sports Collection brings together five arcade titles that launched between 1992 and 1995. Squash, World Rally 1 and 2, Touch and Go, and Snowboarding Champions make up the lineup. text:
Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director explains why the game is sticking with Unreal Engine 4 instead of 5

Naoki Hamaguchi, the director of Final Fantasy 7 Revelation, has spoken about the decision to continue using Unreal Engine 4 for the game instead of Unreal Engine 5. text:
Indie shoot em’ up Viral Reload EX launches onto Nintendo Switch next week

Publisher Flynn’s Arcade has just announced that they’ll be bringing Viral Reload EX to the Nintendo Switch. The title will launch onto the eShop on June 23, 2026.
